name: Asthma
summary: Chronic inflammatory airway disease characterized by variable airflow obstruction, bronchial hyperresponsiveness, mucus hypersecretion, eosinophilic inflammation, and airway remodeling.
learningGoals: Recognize outpatient, acute-care, and toxicology presentations of asthma-related disease.
Connect pathology and airway remodeling to obstructive physiology.
Explain beta2 agonist, muscarinic antagonist, corticosteroid, and methylxanthine pharmacology.
Interpret question-bank style asthma scenarios and adverse-effect patterns.
Use resources and cases to connect active recall with foundational study content.

name: Beta-Blocker Therapy in Cardiovascular Disease
summary: Focused cardiovascular module on beta-adrenergic signaling, cardiac histology, and clinical beta-blocker use for hypertension, angina, and rhythm control.
learningGoals: Identify beta1 receptor biology in cardiac myocytes and connect it to sympathetic physiology.
Recognize core cardiac muscle histology findings including striations, centrally placed nuclei, and intercalated discs.
Explain how metoprolol antagonizes beta1 receptors to reduce heart rate, contractility, cardiac output, and blood pressure.
Apply beta-blocker pharmacology to common clinical indications such as hypertension, angina, and selected tachyarrhythmias.
Use adverse-effect and contraindication reasoning to choose safer beta-blocker strategies in comorbid disease.
